description:
<title>Apply a configuration patch and record the outcome</title>

    <description>
        You are a QA engineer preparing a clean test environment for an upcoming release.  
        In the home directory there is already a small toy project whose configuration must be updated
        by applying a unified diff patch.  
        Afterwards you have to record what you did in a very small, strictly-formatted log file so that
        the continuous-integration system can parse it automatically.

        Preconditions that already exist when the session starts:
          • Directory: /home/user/webapp  
          • File:      /home/user/webapp/settings.conf            (the “old” configuration)  
          • Directory: /home/user/patches  
          • File:      /home/user/patches/settings.patch          (the patch you must apply)  

        Your goals:

          1. Create a backup copy of the original file named
                 /home/user/webapp/settings.conf.bak
             in the same directory.

          2. Apply the patch stored in
                 /home/user/patches/settings.patch
             to /home/user/webapp/settings.conf.

          3. Verify that the file now contains the patched values
             (for example, the port number must be 9090 instead of 8080).

          4. Write a log file named
                 /home/user/patch_logs/apply_settings_patch.log
             following *exactly* this format (4 physical lines, the last one is blank):

                 TIMESTAMP: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
                 ACTION: applied /home/user/patches/settings.patch to /home/user/webapp/settings.conf
                 RESULT: success
                 
             • “TIMESTAMP” must be the current UTC time at the moment the patch is applied,
               formatted exactly as shown (24-hour clock, leading zeros, single space between date and time).
             • “RESULT” must be the word “success” only if the file really ended up patched;
               otherwise write “failure”.
             • The fourth line must be completely empty (just a newline).

        Hints & constraints:

          • Do NOT hard-code the timestamp; generate it when the patch is applied.
          • You do NOT need to show the content of the files on screen, but feel free to
            inspect them to be sure everything worked.
          • You do not have super-user privileges; stay in /home/user.
          • Only the above log file will be parsed by the automated checker, so keep its
            formatting exact—any additional characters (extra spaces, missing newline,
            etc.) will cause the check to fail.

        You should need no more than 3–4 terminal commands in total to finish the work.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
    The automated test suite will check the following final state:

    1. /home/user/webapp/settings.conf.bak must exist and be byte-for-byte identical to the original file:
       ------------------------------------
       # WebApp configuration
       HOST=localhost
       PORT=8080
       DEBUG=false
       TIMEOUT=30
       ------------------------------------

    2. /home/user/webapp/settings.conf must contain the patched configuration:
       ------------------------------------
       # WebApp configuration
       HOST=localhost
       PORT=9090
       DEBUG=true
       TIMEOUT=30
       MAX_CONNECTIONS=100
       ------------------------------------

    3. The patch file that is pre-populated before the task starts:
       Path: /home/user/patches/settings.patch
       Content:
       ------------------------------------
       --- a/settings.conf
       +++ b/settings.conf
       @@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
       +# WebApp configuration
        HOST=localhost
       -PORT=8080
       -DEBUG=false
       +PORT=9090
       +DEBUG=true
        TIMEOUT=30
       +MAX_CONNECTIONS=100
       ------------------------------------

    4. Log file must exist at /home/user/patch_logs/apply_settings_patch.log
       and have exactly 4 lines, pattern:
         Line 1 must match regex   ^TIMESTAMP: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}$
         Line 2 must equal         ACTION: applied /home/user/patches/settings.patch to /home/user/webapp/settings.conf
         Line 3 must equal         RESULT: success
         Line 4 must be blank      (zero characters other than newline)

    5. Directory /home/user/patch_logs must exist (agent should create it).

    Initial file tree that exists before the agent starts:

        /home/user
        ├── webapp
        │   └── settings.conf   (original content shown in #1)
        └── patches
            └── settings.patch  (content shown in #3)

    -->
name: task_000000_74d1c770
